card_description: Asklepios (Greek (uncertain): "Cut open") is a divine or mythological figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Asklepios is god of healing; son of apollo. PKD identifies Asklepios as a healing guide figure in his visions. Appears in 4 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick identifies the pineal gland as the "third eye" and the biological receptor for the "Other Sun" (Sun of Righteousness)."
